forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a680
+project: diagnostics
+title: diagnose(paths) core API with capped broken-dependents
+---
+## What
+The single entry point both consumers (the `diagnostics` tool and the `files edit` op) call. In `swissarmyhammer-diagnostics`:
+
+- `async fn diagnose(session, paths, config) -> DiagnosticsReport`: ensure each path's document is open/synced via the shared `LspSession`, run the settle engine, map to `DiagnosticRecord`s, apply severity filter + per-report cap.
+- **Report sharply** (design "compute freely, surface selectively"): always include the edited/queried file; of its dependents, include only those that *actually broke*. Compute the one-hop blast radius via code-context (`get_blastradius` / inbound callers) — blast radius is cheap, the saved turn is not — then run diagnostics on those dependents and keep only files with new error/warning-severity diagnostics, ranked and capped. No project-wide dump, no unrelated standing warnings.
+- This introduces a `swissarmyhammer-diagnostics → swissarmyhammer-code-context` dependency for blast radius (allowed: diagnostics sits above code-context).
+- Respect `dependents?` toggle and `severity?`/`settle_ms?` overrides in config.
+
+## Depends on
+- "Settle/debounce engine for diagnostics quiescence"
+- "Rewire code-context onto the shared LSP session; collapse its get_diagnostics" (shared session + code-context consuming it)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `diagnose(paths)` returns a `DiagnosticsReport` for the given files, settled, severity-filtered, capped.
+- [ ] Edited file always present; only *broken* one-hop dependents folded in, ranked + capped; clean dependents excluded.
+- [ ] `dependents?`/`severity?`/`settle_ms?` honored.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics`: with a mock session emitting known diagnostics + a stub blast-radius, assert the report includes the target and only broken dependents, respects the cap, and drops clean dependents. Model-free, <1s.
+- [ ] Integration (gated on rust-analyzer): a fixture crate where editing file A breaks dependent B; assert `diagnose([A])` reports A's error and B-as-broken, and does NOT report an unrelated clean file C.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the broken-vs-clean-dependent selection test first.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,6 +6,24 @@
   id: 01kvdahr5cfwg13r1w9n0hrz7m
   text: 'Picked up. Settle engine dep is done (committed 22e6d8e0f). Now researching: (1) code-context''s blast-radius / inbound-callers public API to compute one-hop dependents; (2) how an LspSession is shared so diagnose(session, paths, config) can open/sync docs + settle; (3) confirming diagnostics->code-context dep adds no cycle (code-context must not depend on diagnostics).'
   timestamp: 2026-06-18T12:16:01.196692+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvdbbk5bqjygkcp9svgq84ak
+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed; moving to review.
+
+    New module crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/diagnose.rs:
+    - `Dependents` trait (one-hop blast-radius seam) + `BlastRadiusDependents` production resolver wrapping code-context `get_blastradius(max_hops=1)` (honors the diagnostics->code-context dep; verified no cycle — code-context does not depend on diagnostics).
+    - `async fn diagnose(session, paths, config, dependents, timer) -> DiagnosticsReport`: dedup targets, compute one-hop dependents (gated on config.include_dependents, excluding targets), best-effort sync docs (gated on session.is_running()), settle ALL watched uris (uncapped clone so partitioning happens before the cap), then build_report: targets always included in query order; dependents folded in only if broken (>=1 Error/Warning), ranked errors-desc/warnings-desc/path-asc; truncate to per_report_cap (targets-first). Pending settle -> empty report (no mid-flow snapshot).
+    - Config: added `include_dependents: bool` (default true) for the `dependents?` toggle; `severity?`->severities and `settle_ms?`->settle_window honored via the settle_config (only per_report_cap overridden).
+    - Shared test_support.rs (ManualTimer + NullTransport) extracted and now used by BOTH settle and diagnose tests (removed the duplicated copies the prior reviewer flagged-class).
+    - lsp: added shared `file_uri_from_path` next to `file_path_from_uri` (exact inverses).
+
+    Tests (model-free, <0.01s): 5 diagnose tests — target+broken-dependents/drops-clean, toggle-off, cap-keeps-targets-first, ranking-by-severity, info-only-not-broken. Full crate: 38 passed/0 failed; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
+
+    really-done + adversarial double-check: PASS. The double-check traced build_report on all axes (cannot drop target, cannot include clean/unrelated, ranking correct), confirmed the severity-filter<->broken-check interaction is correct & intended, no dep cycle, path/uri round-trip holds, Pending->empty + is_running gating correct, and the timer-drive test pattern is deterministic (no flake). Fixed its one doc nit (clarified warning-only-dependent-invisible-when-severities-excludes-Warning).
+
+    Deferred: the rust-analyzer e2e integration test (Tests item) was split into follow-up task ^affvtqf — it needs the full code-context indexing pipeline stood up (to populate call edges so A's blast radius includes B) plus rust-analyzer, disproportionate to fold inline and environment-gated. All 3 acceptance CRITERIA are met and unit-covered here.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-18T12:30:08.043616+00:00
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '8180'
 project: diagnostics

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics`: with a mock session emitting known diagnostics + a stub blast-radius, assert the report includes the target and only broken dependents, respects the cap, and drops clean dependents. Model-free, <1s.
